steps.title: Separate fixed and recurring costs
steps.content: The 40-dollar activation fee is paid only once, so it is a constant. The 18-dollar monthly cost is multiplied by the number of months $t$, contributing $18t$.

steps.title: Combine into a total-cost equation
steps.content: Total cost is fixed plus recurring: $c = 18t + 40$.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $c=\frac{t}{18}+40$ — uses $\frac{1}{18}$ dollar per month instead of 18 dollars per month. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: $c=\frac{t}{40}+18$ — 18 dollars becomes the constant and 40 dollars becomes a divisor. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: $c=18t+40$ — correctly adds 18 dollars per month to the one-time 40-dollar fee. Matches.
  • Choice D: $c=40t+18$ — swaps the monthly rate and the activation fee. Eliminated.
That matches Choice C — $c=18t+40$.
